problem_statement: The document examines whether AI coding agents introduce or remove quality issues (code smells) in build system code, and how readily developers accept and merge AI-generated build PRs with little human review.
magnitude_evidence: Analysis of 387 PRs and 945 build files found 364 code smells introduced across 66 files; the AIDev dataset comprises ~933k agentic PRs generated by five AI agents
insufficiency_evidence: more than 61% (238/387) of Agentic-PRs are approved and merged with minimal human intervention, often merged immediately after automated checks passed with no additional modifications and a simple 'LGTM'
solution_mechanisms: manual PR labeling and consensus review
refactoring to remove smells (Externalize Properties, Pull Up Module, Extract and Pull-Up Variable)
developer vetting of AI build changes
smell-aware and refactoring-guided AI agent behavior
